behaviors.description: Scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in rule-engine + merchant-category config wiring (including config lookup and threshold application), which a pure unit mock can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real scoring pipeline with real rule/config modules (no mocked threshold lookup), using a transaction just above the category threshold, and assert the emitted numeric risk score matches expected weighted score contribution.

behaviors.description: Risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: This asserts model judgment quality (fraud-pattern recognition), not just plumbing. Correctness depends on real model inference behavior, which mocking would hide.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative transaction-context sequences to the real risk model endpoint and assert fraud probability crosses the fraud-flag threshold for the pattern cohort while rule-threshold features remain individually below scoring cutoffs.

behaviors.description: Velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly come from timestamp/window filtering and per-user query logic across modules/storage; an in-process mock can miss boundary/time semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test datastore with per-user transactions around the window boundary, execute the real velocity-check component through scoring flow, and assert counted transactions exactly match configured inclusive/exclusive window rules.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review (OR gate, not AND).
behaviors.justification: The critical failure boundary is model-output aggregation + hold decision wiring. Pure unit boolean tests can miss contract/field-mapping issues between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run decision orchestration with real model-output schemas: provide a case where scoring result is below hold threshold and risk model marks fraud; assert review-hold status is true and decision reason indicates risk-model trigger.

behaviors.description: Combined output includes both numeric risk score and risk-model fraud probability so reviewers can see which model flagged and why.
behaviors.justification: This is an output-contract/composition guarantee across scoring, risk, and response assembly modules; mocks can hide serialization/field-loss defects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end local service path (ingest transaction -> score + risk inference stubs or deterministic fixture -> response serializer) and assert response contains numeric score, fraud probability, and per-model flag provenance fields.
